Global (All).summary: The 2023 Israel-Hamas conflict escalated, killing at least 44 people on 5 July 2023.
A suspected US drone strike killed at least six civilians in eastern Afghanistan on June 30, 2023.
Turkey's military operation in Northern Syria resulted in a 50% increase in Syrian refugees arriving in Greece.

United States.title: US Senate passes landmark climate deal
United States.summary: US lawmakers have voted to advance a historic climate bill, setting the country on a path to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

United States.title: Taliban claims responsibility for US drone strikes in Afghanistan
United States.summary: The Taliban has claimed responsibility for US drone strikes in Afghanistan, escalating tensions between the two nations.

United States.title: Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade, sparking abortion rights protests across US
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court has overturned the landmark Roe v. Wade decision, sparking widespread protests and unrest over abortion rights.

South Korea.title: North Korea's Missile Test Raises Tensions
South Korea.summary: On June 30, 2024, North Korea conducted a ballistic missile test, which South Korea and the US condemned as a provocation. The test has raised tensions in the region and increased concerns about the potential for conflict.

South Korea.title: South Korean Military Scandal Under Investigation
South Korea.summary: On June 26, 2024, it was revealed that a high-ranking South Korean military officer was involved in a corruption scandal, leading to an investigation and potentially affecting the country's military readiness.

South Korea.title: Protests Against Government's Response to COVID-19
South Korea.summary: Over the past week, protests have been held in cities across South Korea to express dissatisfaction with the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. The protests have been largely peaceful, but have raised concerns about social cohesion and public anger.

Japan.title: Japan's PM Kishida faces criticism over COVID-19 response
Japan.summary: Critics are questioning Japan's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, with some accusing PM Kishida of being slow to respond to the crisis

Japan.title: North Korea fires multiple ballistic missiles into sea off Japan
Japan.summary: Tensions between North Korea and Japan escalate as Tokyo detects four ballistic missiles fired by Pyongyang into the Sea of Japan

Japan.title: Japan's economy shrinks for first time since 2011
Japan.summary: Japan's economy contracts for the first time since the 2011 tsunami, raising concerns about the country's economic stability
